name: Islamic Educational Foundation
mission: ESTABLISH AND OPERATE PRE-SCHOOL, ELEMENTARY, HIGH SCHOOL.
theoryOfChange: The organization states it cultivates civic-minded, professionally successful, God-conscious Muslim leaders through a private Islamic school system that integrates faith and character development with academic excellence (STEM and the Arts), including Islamic Studies and Quranic Studies focused on memorization plus understanding and practical application.

score_details.score_summary: Islamic Education Foundation of New Jersey Inc shows good impact and limited alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: No explicit mention of "Islamic Educational Foundation" accepting zakat donations was found in the search results. The search results refer to other organizations such as "Islamic Foundation", "Islamic Relief USA", "The Citizens Foundation (TCF) - USA", "Islamic-American Zakat Foundation", and "Crescent Academy International". (Source: https://www.islamicfoundation.org/donate)

all_citations.claim: The organization's mission involves operating schools and its financial profile is tracked via public metadata.
The foundation reported total revenue of $5,631,488, a program expense ratio of 71.5%, and operates Al-Ghazaly High School and Al-Hikmah Elementary School.
The charity provides options for general donations and sadaqah to support its educational programs.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate score reflecting strong financial stability and efficient fundraising, balanced against a lack of third-party impact verification and a specialized religious focus.
areas_for_improvement: Limited public documentation regarding specific program outcomes and impact metrics
Lower alignment with broad donor preferences due to specific religious and cultural focus

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization serves a specific religious and cultural niche, which may result in lower alignment for general donors [1]. As a provider of religious education, it offers a platform for sadaqah-eligible contributions but lacks the broad urgency or universal donor fit associated with humanitarian relief [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates high organizational capacity with a total revenue of $5,631,488 and a program expense ratio of 71.5% [2]. While it maintains a robust financial position with 16.3 months of working capital, there is limited third-party data available to verify specific educational outcomes [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Islamic Education Foundation of New Jersey Inc provides comprehensive faith-based education through its network of elementary and high school programs.
strengths: Maintains a significant working capital reserve of 16.3 months
High fundraising efficiency with $0.00 spent per $1 raised
Established infrastructure for both elementary and secondary education
baseline_narrative.summary: The Islamic Education Foundation of New Jersey Inc is a religious educational organization dedicated to establishing and operating pre-school, elementary, and high school institutions [1]. The foundation manages Al-Ghazaly High School and Al-Hikmah Elementary School to serve the academic and spiritual needs of the community [2]. Supporters can contribute to these educational initiatives through various sadaqah giving options [3].

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The grade is supported by <cite id="10">Cognia accreditation</cite> and consistent <cite id="19">instructional day tracking (179-183 days)</cite>.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The foundation creates impact by providing a structured environment where <cite id="2">secular and religious education</cite> are integrated to produce academically capable and spiritually grounded graduates.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Accreditation ensures that the educational services meet national standards for quality and student achievement.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While the foundation has <cite id="9">strong reserves</cite>, expanding its <cite id="2">STEM and Islamic studies programs</cite> requires ongoing community investment.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="5">71.5% of its expenses toward programs</cite> and maintains a <cite id="8">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>.
IEF maintains <cite id="9">16.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant safety net for long-term institutional stability.
The school system is <cite id="10">accredited by Cognia</cite> with a high score of 352, placing it among the <cite id="11">top 20% of private schools</cite> nationally.

strengths_deep_dive: The Islamic Education Foundation of New Jersey stands out for its institutional maturity and academic excellence. Having <cite id="1">operated for over 40 years</cite>, it has successfully scaled its revenue with a <cite id="12">3-year CAGR of 12.5%</cite>. Its academic rigor is validated by external benchmarks, including being ranked in the <cite id="13">top 20% of private STEM high schools</cite> in the country. This combination of financial growth and academic validation suggests a highly stable and effective educational environment.
rich_narrative.summary: The Islamic Education Foundation of New Jersey Inc (IEF) is a long-standing educational institution that has been <cite id="1">operating since 1984</cite> to provide comprehensive faith-based schooling. The organization manages a robust academic infrastructure, including <cite id="2">Al-Ghazaly High School and Al-Hikmah Elementary School</cite>, which integrate rigorous secular academics with Islamic studies. Financially, the foundation demonstrates significant scale for a regional educational body, reporting <cite id="3">total revenue of $4,637,834</cite> in the 2023 tax year. A defining characteristic of IEF's financial model is its heavy reliance on <cite id="4">program service revenue, which accounts for $4,279,911</cite> of its total income, reflecting a sustainable tuition-based model supplemented by community support. The foundation maintains an <cite id="5">exceptional program expense ratio of 71.5%</cite>, indicating that all reported functional expenses are directed toward its educational mission. Furthermore, the organization operates with high efficiency, reporting <cite id="6">$0.00 spent on fundraising for every $1 raised</cite>. Beyond classroom instruction, the foundation provides specialized tracks such as the <cite id="7">Quranic Studies & Hifth Program</cite> and organizes community-building initiatives like Umrah trips, ensuring a holistic environment for spiritual and intellectual growth.
